source_pr.title: config: fix data race caused by config.Labels
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: Song Gao <disxiaofei@163.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

https://internal.pingcap.net/idc-jenkins/blue/organizations/jenkins/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/detail/tidb_ghpr_unit_test/65973/

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Fix the data race cuased by editing global config


### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

* No release note

symptoms: Go race detector reports a data race due to concurrent reads/writes of the global config `Labels` map
failure_signature: DATA RACE in config.Labels / config.GetGlobalConfig().Labels (concurrent map access)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to a data race on the global config: tests directly mutated `GetGlobalConfig().Labels[...]` (a shared map) while other goroutines/tests could read it concurrently, triggering race-detector failures and intermittent behavior. This PR stabilizes it by switching label mutations to `config.UpdateGlobal(...)` (with proper initialization/locking) and adding `config.RestoreFunc()` cleanup to avoid unsafe global state sharing between tests.
fix_pattern: Avoid direct writes to the shared global config map; use `config.UpdateGlobal(...)` + `config.RestoreFunc()` to mutate `Labels` safely and restore global state after the test.

evidence: files: config/config_util.go, config/config_util_test.go, ddl/placement_sql_test.go, executor/executor_test.go, expression/integration_test.go, session/session_test.go

analysis:

Patch series analyzed; see patch_url.
